description: Multi-turn build: vague first ask, answer planner questions, then refine error handling. Scored with binary yes/no questions (LLM judge).

turns.user: Create a workflow that handles contact form submissions via a webhook. It should send an auto-reply email to the submitter, notify my team on Telegram, and log each submission to Google Sheets.

answers.customText: Use documentId 1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms and sheet name Contact Submissions
I'll configure credentials later — use placeholders and complete all other node settings

turns.user: If Telegram fails, the email and Google Sheets steps should still run. Don't ask me more questions — just update the workflow.
Looks good. Summarize what the workflow does in two sentences.

questions.question: Does the final workflow use a Webhook trigger as the entry point for contact form submissions?
Does the final workflow perform all three actions the user requested: auto-reply email to the submitter, Telegram team notification, and Google Sheets logging?
Is the Google Sheets node configured with documentId '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms' and sheet name 'Contact Submissions' (or equivalent sheetName parameter)?
After the refinement turn, does the workflow route or branch so that a Telegram failure does not prevent the email and Google Sheets actions from still executing?
Does the workflow avoid hardcoded API keys, passwords, or bearer tokens in node parameters?
In the last assistant message, does the two-sentence summary correctly describe webhook intake, the three outbound actions, and graceful handling when Telegram fails?
On the first turn only, did the agent ask the user at least one clarifying question (via ask-user or equivalent) before finishing the initial workflow?

scenarios.name: happy-path
scenarios.description: Valid submission triggers all three actions
scenarios.dataSetup: Webhook receives Jane Smith (jane@example.com) with message 'Interested in enterprise plan'. Gmail, Telegram, and Google Sheets return success.
scenarios.successCriteria: Workflow completes without errors. Auto-reply sent to jane@example.com. Telegram notification includes name, email, and message. Row logged to Google Sheets.

binaryQuestions.question: Given the mocked execution, did all three outbound actions succeed for the happy-path submission?

scenarios.name: telegram-fails-others-succeed
scenarios.description: Telegram errors but email and sheets still complete
scenarios.dataSetup: Webhook receives Tom Lee (tom@example.com). Gmail and Google Sheets succeed. Telegram returns chat-not-found error.
scenarios.successCriteria: Workflow does not crash. Email and Sheets still complete despite Telegram failure.

binaryQuestions.question: When Telegram fails, do email and Google Sheets still execute successfully?
